id.description: Universally unique identifier of the event. To allow for reliable retransmission, only the earliest received event of a given UUID is considered valid by the server and subsequent are ignored.

action.description: The type of activity this represents.

x-enumDescriptions.VIEW: Represents a visit to the given `url`.
x-enumDescriptions.EDIT: Represents an edit of the document represented by the `url`.
x-enumDescriptions.SEARCH: Represents a search performed at the given `url`.
x-enumDescriptions.COMMENT: Represents a comment on the document represented by the `url`.
x-enumDescriptions.CRAWL: Represents an explicit request to index the given `url` along with associated attributes in this payload.
x-enumDescriptions.HISTORICAL_SEARCH: Represents a search performed at the given `url` as indicated by the user's history.
x-enumDescriptions.HISTORICAL_VIEW: Represents a visit to the given `url` as indicated by the user's history.

x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.VIEW: Represents a visit to the given `url`.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.EDIT: Represents an edit of the document represented by the `url`.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.SEARCH: Represents a search performed at the given `url`.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.COMMENT: Represents a comment on the document represented by the `url`.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.CRAWL: Represents an explicit request to index the given `url` along with associated attributes in this payload.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.HISTORICAL_SEARCH: Represents a search performed at the given `url` as indicated by the user's history.
x-speakeasy-enum-descriptions.HISTORICAL_VIEW: Represents a visit to the given `url` as indicated by the user's history.

bodyContent.description: The HTML content of the page body.

datasourceInstance.description: The full datasource instance name inferred from the URL of the event

datasource.description: The datasource without the instance inferred from the URL of the event

instanceOnlyName.description: The instance only name of the datasource instance, e.g. 1 for jira_1, inferred from the URL of the event

duration.description: Length in seconds of the activity. For VIEWS, this represents the amount the page was visible in the foreground.

query.description: The user's search query associated with a SEARCH.

referrer.description: The referring URL of the VIEW or SEARCH.

title.description: The page title associated with the URL of the event

truncated.description: Indicates that the parameters are incomplete and more parameters may be sent with the same action+timestamp+URL in the future. This is used for sending the duration when a `VIEW` is finished.

timestamp.description: The ISO 8601 timestamp when the activity began.
